folders.label: Curriculum by myself
folders.title: Curriculum by myself
folders.description: Currently working with China Soong Ching Ling Youth Science and Technology and Culture Exchange Center, a public institution directly under the China Soong Ching Ling Foundation. It consists of five business departments: experience center, art center, preschool education center, performing arts center, and exchange center. , build the three functions of the world youth exchange and mutual learning platform, and actively carry out domestic and international youth science and technology cultural exchange activities.

content.title: Job title: Creative animation teacher 2021-Present
content.description:
I think all kinds of painting have a set of theories to support, and theoretical knowledge is primarily dull for children. My curriculum design is mainly to let children learn to 'love.' The meaning of 'love' can be understood as love of painting, love of life, love of the world, and so on. With love, children can explore on their own. The learning and mastering of all theories are based on experience. Experience is a process accumulated over a long time, so I think 'love' is the most important thing.
My course content is mainly based on observing life, starting from small objects and things around me, combined with a rich game process and multi-sensory experience. We will work together to complete paintings in class, listen to music, watch videos, and observe objects around us with a magnifying glass, hoping to bring children an immersive classroom experience.

In the classroom, we completed it with cooperation, each person only drew a color block, and then passed it on until each student left traces on different papers. When the children got their own work, it was beyond recognition. So, let's think about the relationship between colors, and each color is crowded, will they have different emotions?

In the class, we listened to the sounds of different planets, observed the surface of the planets, and heard the children's sharing of their own understanding of outer space. Guide children to create their own planet, spacecraft and cute aliens

During the course, we observed the underwater world and listened to the sound of seawater. Guide the children to tear up the reference map, re-collage, and break the stereotype of the traditional reference map of chess - copying. Based on the collaged works, detailed fragment observation is carried out. Destroying the original shape makes it easier for children to enter into careful observation.

content.title: Job title: Fashion Design Course Teacher 2020-2021
content.description: Work with private art training institutions, teaching fashion design courses. Costume design courses for children should be fun. Children choose to be exposed to costume design because they want to try more different materials and love dress-up games. Therefore, in the curriculum setting, I threw away a lot of theories and measurement data and guided children to create clothes with their thinking. The course includes hand-painted clothing renderings and fabric transformations and teaching other fabric products.

text2.title: Clothing rendering course, using a variety of creative methods: hand-painted coloring, collage coloring, etc.

text.title: The color matching course encourages students to choose their clothes and understand the principles of color matching.

content.title: Job title: Seal Carving Technique Teacher Short Course

folders.title: School-based Curriculum
folders.description: From 2017 to 2020, work in public kindergartens and primary and secondary schools different from Beijing. According to the different semester settings of each school and referring to the school training plan, complete the teaching of various courses, including traditional Chinese painting, oil painting, sketch, children's creative painting, etc.

content.title: A Note of Gratitude
content.description: I would like to thank every school and teacher for their trust in me, students' cooperation and parents' support. It has been five years since I first stood on the podium. Thank you for accompanying the growth of a teacher. I hope to bring better courses to students through continuous learning.

folders.description: I learned different art forms. Learn different artistic creation tools. At the same time, for personal creation, start thinking about the project creation of the theme system, and connect your feelings with creation, the feelings of painting objects, the feelings of the times, and the feelings of society.

content.title: My World
content.description: Art creation starts from personal feelings, exploring different worlds based on my different latitudes. It is about: Taste world ( the first world of sense), the Real-world ( the second world in parallel), the Dark world ( the third world of the underground), the Ideal world( the fourth world of future), and the Minds world ( the fifth world of nothingness). Each work is completed through illustration with the drawing of color pencils, font design, poetry creation, graphic design, and so on.

folders.label: Art Practice
folders.title: Art Practice
folders.description: A personal meditation on identity — 'Who I am' expressed not through biography but through fragmented numerical codes, each a quiet abstraction of the self rendered across a series of digital and mixed-media works.

content.title: Who I am
content.description:
337**082 · 239005****10013120 · 0735**8421 · 0769**704 · 1590***1563 · 282**9985

Identity distilled into redacted numbers — ID cards, phone numbers, postal codes, student records. The self as data, partially obscured, partially revealed.

folders.label: Today Art Museum
folders.title: Today Art Museum — Public Education
folders.description: Beijing Today Art Museum is the first private non-enterprise public welfare Contemporary Art Museum in China, founded by Mr. Zhang Baoquan in 2002 and transformed into a non-profit in 2006. Over 18 years, it has actively explored sustainable development for China's private art museums, building a stable financing mechanism and academic system.

content.title: Exhibition to Perform
content.description: I am thrilled to join the work of Today Art Museum. Exhibition execution is a big part of my daily work. Participating in the execution and planning of the exhibition has enabled me to understand the presentation process of an exhibition fully. From exhibition planning seminars to material procurement to on-site execution, every part is a new challenge for me, and I enjoy it very much. It gives me strength every day.

content.title: Participate in exhibition training and exhibition launches.
Learn equipment use and exhibit maintenance from workers.
Visit Artist Studio
content.description: I participated in the execution and planning of dozens of exhibitions during the work period.

content.title: Public Education
content.description: Independently design exhibition public education courses, complete exhibition explanation, course activity planning, classroom layout, course implementation, and other related work. Serve as a course lecturer and teaching assistant teacher and take photography during course activities.

text.title: Course in Action
text.description: Designing and leading public education programmes tied directly to the museum's rotating exhibitions.

content.title: According to the exhibition's content, design exhibition courses suitable for children.
Graphic Design
content.description: Produce promotional materials during the exhibition, exhibition brochures, and graphic design of peripheral products. Work on course facts, full exhibition presentations, artist interviews, and more.

content.title: A Note of Gratitude
content.description: I would like to thank the Today Art Museum for giving me this job opportunity. Every challenging job has given me many harvests. This memory is unforgettable to this day. After this job, I discovered my love for contemporary art and education. Thanks to every leader and colleague for helping me grow. This work experience is unforgettable to this day.

folders.label: Project 1
folders.title: What's Bothering You?
folders.description: Creation aims to explore the inner troubles of the subjects — the reality or illusion that is difficult to get rid of. Concrete or abstract things that disturb the heart and make it uneasy. The shooting process invites each subject to get along with their troubles, facing the things or illusions that perplex them. Exploring changes in their behaviour, heart, and spirit.

content.title: Subject: CICI — Accountancy
content.description:
There are many books, but she has no time to read them. There is more than a little gap between what we want to do and what we do. Adults are troubled by 'time' most of the time. Facing a mountain of books she could not start, yet she kept buying more. Watching them accumulate dust and fade, she fell into deep remorse.

Shooting equipment: PENTAX ESPIO80 · Film: ILFORD PAN400

content.title: Subject: Zoe — Makeup Artist
content.description:
Entangled in work. The content is to make customers beautiful, but her original intention for learning makeup was to make herself beautiful. In the process of work, she drifted further and further from that original intention.

Shooting equipment: PENTAX ESPIO80 · Film: ILFORD PAN400

content.title: Subject: Zhua — Student
content.description:
Heavy academic pressure causes acne on her face, and sometimes her whole body breaks out in allergic reactions due to stress. Graduate coursework has shaken her confidence countless times, and the physical symptoms led to thoughts of dropping out.

Shooting equipment: PENTAX ESPIO80 · Film: ILFORD PAN400

folders.label: Exhibition Sharing
folders.title: David Altmejd
folders.description: David Altmejd's portrait heads and busts of fantastical, hybrid human and animal characters encompass elements of uncanny naturalism combined with raw and provisional qualities — uniting resin, plaster and foam with glass eyes, crystal encrustations, and hanks of hair. His sculptures appear always in flux: arrested in a cycle of growth, division, or decay.

content.title: The Trickster
content.description: The presiding spirit of the exhibition is the hare, associated with the Jungian archetype of the Trickster — irrational and capricious, a prankster and shapeshifter. Trickster appears across cultures as Hermes the messenger, Loki the gender-fluid master of disguise, Eshu in Yoruba mythology, and Rabbit to the peoples of the Great Plains. Here the Hare, alter-ego for the artist, assumes many forms: cartoon-like or endowed with human features and emotions; ears reduced to symbolic stumps or enlarged as a canvas for drawings; even metamorphosing into snake, bat, or whale. Acting as his own analyst, the artist identifies this crowd of characters as manifestations of different aspects of his personality — allowing us to perceive the exhibition as a multi-faceted psychic self-portrait.

content.title: Material to Ethereal
content.description:
On the ground floor of the gallery we encounter a human figure with the ears of a hare, seated in yogic pose. Its giant ears, stretching almost to the ceiling, seem to probe the limits of the room, while in front of it is a burrow from which the figure appears to have excavated the very matter from which it is made. The contrast of feet of clay and ears spread like dragonfly wings suggests a transformation in progress — from the material to the ethereal.

Altmejd has always sought to absolve his conscious mind from the responsibility of creation, instead attributing his sculptures with their own agency. The crystals he often uses are, in the sculptor's lexicon, an energy source with which the works are charged. So the Trickster — mercurial catalyst for transformation, messenger from the subconscious — presents himself as a welcome proxy for the artist, freeing his imagination and spurring it to wilder transformations.

Trickster's shapeshifting powers are truly unleashed across the lower gallery's rows of busts and heads. Caught mid-metamorphosis, an elegant hare grows lizard scales, ears transform to leathery batwings, and a belly swells with the sleek curves of a killer whale. The most human of the company are given archetypal designations: The Magician, The Other, Young Man, The Mother.

folders.label: Independent Studies

folders.description: Sessions 3: Theory — Practice — Research KF. A focused independent study unit exploring the relationships between theoretical frameworks, studio practice, and research methodology.

content.title: Sessions 3: Theory — Practice — Research KF
content.description: An independent study exploring the interconnections between theory, practice, and research. Each piece in this series reflects a distinct stage of that enquiry — from conceptual grounding to material experimentation to reflective analysis.
